packages.expected_behavior: JiYong/Tars or authorized owner approves architecture, dependency scope, migration/rollback plan, and security ownership.
packages.expected_evidence: Dated APPROVE/REJECT record with scope, exceptions, rollback owner, dependency decision.
packages.given: VS3 architecture adds or changes authz, tenant isolation, durable migration, OPA, sandbox, registry, signing, or production dependencies.

redaction_rules.allowed_evidence_shape: redacted transcript, signed approval/rejection, scope, findings, and stable refs
redaction_rules.required_redaction_note: Reviewer must state what was redacted and why raw secrets or tokens are absent.

reject_conditions: No named owner for tenant isolation, OPA/policy, registry/signing, or migration rollback.
Approval text grants production readiness or VS3-P without the remaining H-row evidence.
Dependency, authz, sandbox, or rollback scope is materially changed without review.
packages.release_impact: Remains HUMAN_REQUIRED until signed owner evidence exists; blocks VS3-P and security-sensitive implementation gates as applicable.
review_checklist: Review the final VS3-L aggregate report and the final regression proof before any approval decision.
Confirm the architecture ownership, dependency scope, migration/rollback owner, and security owner are named.
Record each exception or deferred risk explicitly instead of granting a blanket approval.
packages.why_ai_cannot_verify: This row requires a person, real external system, production/on-prem topology, signed review, or subjective operator acceptance outside local deterministic proof.

packages.expected_behavior: Independent reviewer validates tenant, policy, connector, tool, extension, and egress bypass resistance.
packages.expected_evidence: Signed review, test scope/topology, findings, remediation, retest evidence.
packages.given: Representative production/on-prem topology is ready for adversarial review.

redaction_rules.allowed_evidence_shape: redacted transcript, signed approval/rejection, scope, findings, and stable refs
redaction_rules.required_redaction_note: Reviewer must state what was redacted and why raw secrets or tokens are absent.

reject_conditions: Review omits any of tenant isolation, policy, connector authority, tool runtime, extension activation, or egress.
Open release-blocking finding lacks remediation and retest evidence.
The topology differs from the one intended for VS3-P/on-prem candidate use.
packages.release_impact: Remains HUMAN_REQUIRED until independent evidence exists; blocks VS3-P.
review_checklist: Attach the representative topology and the reviewed attack paths before recording the decision.
Validate tenant, policy, connector, tool, extension, and egress bypass resistance in the same review scope.
Record findings, remediation, and retest evidence as separate evidence refs.
packages.when: Independent security review and retest occur.
packages.why_ai_cannot_verify: This row requires a person, real external system, production/on-prem topology, signed review, or subjective operator acceptance outside local deterministic proof.

packages.expected_behavior: Real user/group/role/attribute/revocation mapping is validated without promoting synthetic fixture proof.
packages.expected_evidence: Redacted login, mapping, revocation transcript, owner/security approval.
packages.given: Real OIDC/SSO or enterprise identity provider is selected.

redaction_rules.allowed_evidence_shape: redacted transcript, signed approval/rejection, scope, findings, and stable refs
redaction_rules.required_redaction_note: Reviewer must state what was redacted and why raw secrets or tokens are absent.

reject_conditions: Synthetic fixture or local-only identity proof is presented as real IdP readiness.
Revocation is not tested or does not fail closed.
Transcript exposes raw identity tokens, credentials, or personal data outside the approved scope.
packages.release_impact: Remains HUMAN_REQUIRED until real IdP evidence exists; blocks VS3-P.
review_checklist: Use a real OIDC/SSO or enterprise IdP tenant selected by the owner; synthetic fixtures are not enough.
Capture redacted login, group/role/attribute mapping, and revocation transcripts.
Confirm revoked identity or group membership fails closed across API, CLI, and policy checks where applicable.
packages.why_ai_cannot_verify: This row requires a person, real external system, production/on-prem topology, signed review, or subjective operator acceptance outside local deterministic proof.

packages.expected_behavior: Real topology enforces deny-by-default and approved exceptions outside local harness.
packages.expected_evidence: Topology diagram, firewall/proxy/network-policy evidence, packet/log transcript, approval.
packages.given: Target on-prem network, DNS, proxy, firewall, service mesh, and sandbox are available.

redaction_rules.allowed_evidence_shape: redacted transcript, signed approval/rejection, scope, findings, and stable refs
redaction_rules.required_redaction_note: Reviewer must state what was redacted and why raw secrets or tokens are absent.

reject_conditions: Allowed egress succeeds without a declared sink, policy decision, and audit ref.
Denied path reaches external network, alternate DNS, proxy, socket, or protocol escape.
Topology proof is local harness only while claiming real on-prem network readiness.
packages.release_impact: Remains HUMAN_REQUIRED until real topology evidence exists; blocks VS3-P.
review_checklist: Attach the target network, DNS, proxy, firewall, service-mesh, and sandbox topology before review.
Test deny-by-default and approved exception paths outside the local harness.
Record packet, proxy, firewall, or network-policy evidence with timestamps and redaction notes.
packages.why_ai_cannot_verify: This row requires a person, real external system, production/on-prem topology, signed review, or subjective operator acceptance outside local deterministic proof.

packages.expected_behavior: Low-risk read-only or explicitly approved live provider rehearsal succeeds without secret exposure.
packages.expected_evidence: Redacted provider transcript, approval, ConnectorHub/CornerStone audit refs, result/evidence refs.
packages.given: Live ConnectorHub credentials and provider permissions are available.

redaction_rules.allowed_evidence_shape: redacted transcript, signed approval/rejection, scope, findings, and stable refs
redaction_rules.required_redaction_note: Reviewer must state what was redacted and why raw secrets or tokens are absent.

reject_conditions: Live provider call bypasses ConnectorHub capability or source-policy mediation.
Transcript includes raw provider token, secret, credential, or unredacted personal data.
Provider rehearsal performs mutation without explicit approval and result evidence.
packages.release_impact: Remains HUMAN_REQUIRED until live-provider evidence exists; blocks live readiness claim.
review_checklist: Use only approved live provider credentials and the least-privilege provider permission set.
Run a low-risk read-only or explicitly approved live-provider rehearsal through ConnectorHub boundaries.
Attach redacted provider transcript, ConnectorHub audit refs, CornerStone evidence refs, and zero-secret proof.
packages.why_ai_cannot_verify: This row requires a person, real external system, production/on-prem topology, signed review, or subjective operator acceptance outside local deterministic proof.

packages.expected_behavior: Operator accepts or rejects whether boundaries, causes, risks, and recovery paths are understandable and not misleading.
packages.expected_evidence: ACCEPT/REJECT note, screenshots/recording, task outcomes, issue list if rejected.
packages.given: Human operator uses denial, audit, connector, source-policy, tool registry, activation, and migration/status UX.

redaction_rules.allowed_evidence_shape: redacted transcript, signed approval/rejection, scope, findings, and stable refs
redaction_rules.required_redaction_note: Reviewer must state what was redacted and why raw secrets or tokens are absent.

reject_conditions: Operator cannot identify why a denial, degraded status, or human gate blocks VS3-P.
UX implies human acceptance, production readiness, live provider readiness, or migration readiness without evidence.
Review is based only on generated JSON with no human walkthrough evidence.
packages.release_impact: Remains HUMAN_REQUIRED until human acceptance exists; blocks UX acceptance claim.
review_checklist: Walk through denial, audit, connector, source-policy, tool registry, activation, and migration/status UX.
Record task outcomes, confusing labels, misleading green states, screenshots or recording refs, and accept/reject decision.
Keep subjective acceptance separate from the local DOM/browser regression proof.
packages.why_ai_cannot_verify: This row requires a person, real external system, production/on-prem topology, signed review, or subjective operator acceptance outside local deterministic proof.

packages.expected_behavior: Migration, backup, restore, rollback, quarantine, RLS, policy, and audit drill passes under approved data rules.
packages.expected_evidence: Signed transcript, before/after counts/hashes, restore/rollback result, RLS/policy inventory.
packages.given: Real or representative production-like data, retention rules, backup tooling, and rollback authority are available.

redaction_rules.allowed_evidence_shape: redacted transcript, signed approval/rejection, scope, findings, and stable refs
redaction_rules.required_redaction_note: Reviewer must state what was redacted and why raw secrets or tokens are absent.

reject_conditions: Drill uses destructive production mutation without explicit owner approval.
Before/after counts or hashes do not reconcile and no quarantine or remediation is recorded.
Rollback, restore, RLS, policy, or audit evidence is missing while claiming migration readiness.
packages.release_impact: Remains HUMAN_REQUIRED until signed drill evidence exists; blocks migration/restore readiness claim.
review_checklist: Use approved real or representative production-like data and retention rules before the drill.
Run a non-destructive migration, backup, restore, rollback, quarantine, RLS, policy, and audit drill under supervision.
Record before/after counts, hashes, restore/rollback result, RLS/policy inventory, and operator sign-off.
packages.why_ai_cannot_verify: This row requires a person, real external system, production/on-prem topology, signed review, or subjective operator acceptance outside local deterministic proof.

human_gate_validation_rehearsal.promotion_rule: This rehearsal proves the generated package contracts can be consumed by the native human-gate validator with redacted temporary records. It is not signed human evidence and cannot promote VS3-H rows or unlock VS3-P.

operator_guidance: Local VS3 subsystem proofs are visible separately.
Human gates require signed evidence before VS3-P or acceptance claims.
Degraded components are not collapsed into a green milestone state.

proof_boundary.notes: This proof prepares local operator, audit, and human-gate evidence only; it is not production/on-prem, independent security, or human UX acceptance evidence.
